title: Présent des verbes en -er
lesson: Regular -er verbs are the largest French verb group. Drop the -er and add the present-tense endings: -e (je), -es (tu), -e (il/elle), -ons (nous), -ez (vous), -ent (ils/elles). The -es and -ent endings are silent, so most forms sound the same.

items.explanation: -er verbs: the 'je' form ends in -e: parler → je parle.
-er verbs: the 'tu' form ends in -es: habiter → tu habites.
-er verbs: the 'nous' form ends in -ons: aimer → nous aimons.
-er verbs: the 'ils/elles' form ends in -ent (silent): regarder → ils regardent.
-er verbs: the 'vous' form ends in -ez: manger → vous mangez.
-er verbs: the 'il/elle' form ends in -e: marcher → elle marche.
-er verbs: the 'je' form ends in -e: écouter → j'écoute (note the accent on é).
-er verbs: the 'tu' form ends in -es: parler → tu parles.
-er verbs: the 'nous' form ends in -ons: travailler → nous travaillons.
-er verbs: the 'ils/elles' form ends in -ent (silent): jouer → ils jouent.
-er verbs: the 'vous' form ends in -ez: donner → vous donnez.
